content.text: Two weapons is a stance. Two heavy weapons is a procedure.
Dual Swing: wielding two one-handed weapons, pay the higher weapon's AP and both weapons attack. Once per turn. Until your next turn, your Evasion drops by the combined weight of both weapons.
Both weapons attack in full — each rolls its own accuracy, damage, and tags.
Weights are printed per weapon (see Weapons).
The Dancer ladder reduces the Evasion penalty — see Dancer.
Heavy weapons (4–5 AP) are two-handed. One-handing one requires Chrome 12 for the 4 AP class or Chrome 14 for the 5 AP class. Meat arms don't qualify — that's physics, not policy.
Dual Heavy Swing: pay the higher weapon's AP +1. Both weapons attack with full dice. Your Evasion drops by the combined weight — no floor.
Recoil: every Dual Heavy Swing deals 1 Armor HP to your own worn shell (1 flesh if unarmored). You are grinding your own shell clock.
No reaction attacks while dual-gripping — your hands are full of physics.
Dancer discounts never apply to Heavy pairs. That lane is light blades; this lane is chrome.
physics didn't care that it looked cool. it did look cool though.

content.label: PARKED
content.text: Multi-armed race interaction with Titan Grip is on file pending the race redesign. It is not in play, and nothing on this page prices it.
Simultaneous heavy-weapons operation voids the standard harness warranty. Per-swing structural billing applies. The surgical suite remains available to discuss load-bearing upgrades.
if your own gear is filing damage reports about you, the job is trying to tell you something.
